title: fix(scripts): use millisecond timestamps in seed upgrade cron
author: fisventurous

gist.headline: Time to sprout
gist.blurb: Fixed a tiny time-travel bug that was keeping our newest users from automatically upgrading to the Seed tier. If you've been waiting for your upgrade, your pollen is on the way!
gist.summary: Fixed a timestamp mismatch in the auto-upgrade script that prevented eligible Spore users from being upgraded to the Seed tier.
gist.impact: Users who meet the GitHub activity requirements will now correctly and automatically receive their Seed tier upgrades and increased pollen allowances.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about fixing a time/timestamp issue that prevented 'Spore' users from upgrading to 'Seed' tier. Visuals: The bee mascot (Polly) fixing a giant glowing clock or hourglass in a garden, while little pixelated seeds sprout into small plants. The Monitor Robot is holding a stopwatch or calendar.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, fixing a glowing retro clock in a digital garden where tiny seeds are sprouting into lime green (#ecf874) plants. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holds a stopwatch nearby. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
